course.title: Build and Ship a Safe Evaluated AI Agent with Python 3.14 and Gemini 3.5
course.description: Python, HTTP, prompt, and agent-loop learners who need to design, implement, evaluate, secure, operate, and defend a tool-using Gemini agent instead of copy a brittle while-loop demo or hide core contracts behind a framework. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate python 3.14.6; product and risk contracts; reproducible repositories; gemini interactions api; gemini 3.5 capability and stability selection; google-genai 2.11.0; deterministic provider ports and fakes; typed interaction parts and state; pydantic 2.13.4 and json schema 2020-12 structured output; typed tool schemas, allowlisted dispatch, least agency, and complete function-call circulation; explicit bounded loops; asyncio taskgroup concurrency; human approval and durable resume; session events, checkpoints, replay, context compaction, and poisoning-resistant memory; retry and idempotency; accessible event streaming; owasp-informed injection, exfiltration, memory, tool, identity, code-execution, multi-agent, and denial-of-wallet defense; provider data terms, privacy, retention, and sensitive-use review; deterministic, trajectory, rubric, model-judge, and human-calibrated evaluation; opentelemetry genai observability; token, cost, rate, caching, latency, capacity, and overload control; google-adk 2.4.0 translation and conformance; contained delegation; sandboxed code and computer-use boundaries; packaging, api, deployment, migration, canary, rollback, kill switch, recovery, and production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-python evidence using original fixed fixtures plus explicit provider, model, sdk, http, identity, secret, storage, sandbox, browser, database, telemetry, accessibility, privacy, security, load, cost, and production transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative authentic agent deliveries and a performance-based unfamiliar-agent defense examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves stakeholder task, risk tier, authority, revision, Python, SDK, API and model identity, interaction and call IDs, state and budget, tool policy, approval, validation, accessibility, privacy and security boundary, changed-case test, failure, recovery, and explicit browser-versus-controlled-system limits before adding one agent capability.

Browser Python uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure functions. It does not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; those require controlled evidence.

Passing work requires stable scenario and artifact identity, prediction, intermediate interaction, call, result, state, approval, evaluation, trace, or recovery evidence, exact observable output, a changed and rejected case, a test that detects a deliberate defect, accessible stakeholder evidence, and a named owner for remaining risk.

credential.title: Build and Ship a Safe Evaluated AI Agent with Python 3.14 and Gemini 3.5 Certificate

modules.title: Stakeholder Task, Workflow Fit, Authority, Risk, and Evidence
modules.description: A sponsor asks for an autonomous assistant but has not defined the user decision, accepted failure, permitted actions, affected people, oversight, or why a deterministic workflow is insufficient. Learners cumulatively produce bounded agent product and risk charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define stakeholder, user population, decision, task distribution, observable value, failure, review cadence, and non-goals before choosing a model.
Compare deterministic code, workflow, model-assisted step, and agent loop and choose the least variable architecture that satisfies the task.
Classify impact, reversibility, affected groups, uncertainty, data sensitivity, action authority, and required human oversight.

modules.title: Repository, Python Runtime, SDKs, Dependencies, and Reproduction
modules.description: The prototype runs from an unknown revision with a floating Gemini SDK, a copied deprecated import, hidden environment state, no license inventory, and no clean-install proof. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible Python agent repository baseline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inspect revision, status, existing behavior, tests, configuration, fixtures, secrets, licenses, threat model, and recovery anchor before editing.
Record Python implementation and version, platform, event-loop behavior, SSL backend, free-threaded status, encoding environment, and supported matrix.
Pin direct and resolved dependencies, verify package sources and hashes, review licenses and advisories, and prove isolated installation.

modules.title: Models, Interactions API, Capabilities, Stability, and Provider Ports
modules.description: The code hard-codes a preview model, assumes every endpoint supports tools and schemas, mixes legacy generateContent examples with Interactions state, and has no migration seam. Learners cumulatively produce verified model capability and provider-adapter record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use the Gemini Interactions API for new agent work and document when a legacy generateContent integration must remain during migration.
Discover and record model support for interactions, function calls, structured output, multimodal parts, thinking, streaming, context, and limits.
Separate stable, preview, experimental, deprecated, and retired features and assign migration, fallback, and re-review owners.

modules.title: Deterministic Model Port, Scripted Fake, Recordings, and Test Identity
modules.description: Routine tests call a live model, accept semantic drift as flakiness, overwrite golden responses, and cannot prove which request produced a failure. Learners cumulatively produce scripted provider fake and versioned interaction fixture set through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Express model requests, typed steps, usage, errors, cancellation, and async lifecycle through a minimal Python protocol.
Build a scripted fake that consumes expected requests and emits deterministic model output, tool calls, safety blocks, usage, and faults.
Store sanitized versioned provider fixtures with schema, model, API, SDK, timestamp, purpose, and explicit replay limits.

modules.title: Interaction Resources, Typed Parts, Conversation State, and Retention
modules.description: The agent concatenates messages into text, loses function-call identity, assumes previous_interaction_id preserves tools and system instruction, and cannot explain provider retention. Learners cumulatively produce typed Gemini interaction and state ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model each interaction with stable local identity, provider interaction ID, parent, state mode, timestamps, model, configuration, and outcome.
Preserve text, function call, function result, image, file, refusal, safety, and model-output parts without lossy string flattening.
Use previous_interaction_id deliberately, distinguish server-side history from stateless replay, and preserve local audit evidence.

modules.title: Structured Output, Pydantic, JSON Schema, Validation, and Repair
modules.description: The model returns JSON-looking text that is coerced into domain objects, blocked outputs are parsed as success, schema drift is hidden, and invalid values are silently repaired. Learners cumulatively produce strict versioned agent output boundary through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use structured output for final typed data and function calling for intermediate actions rather than confusing the two contracts.
Validate provider output with strict Pydantic models, explicit bounds, discriminated variants, forbidden extras, and actionable errors.
Generate, inspect, version, and test the supported JSON Schema subset instead of assuming provider and local validators implement every keyword.

modules.title: Typed Tool Contracts, JSON Schema, Descriptions, Domains, and Versions
modules.description: A broad execute tool accepts free-form strings, hides side effects, uses ambiguous parameter names, has no bounds or schema version, and exposes more authority than the task needs. Learners cumulatively produce least-capability typed tool catalog through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define one narrow tool purpose, effect class, caller, user benefit, authority, timeout, idempotency, and prohibited use.
Specify required properties, types, bounds, patterns, enums, nested limits, and additional-property policy with a supported schema subset.
Write tool and parameter descriptions that distinguish when to call, when not to call, required evidence, effects, and failure semantics.

modules.title: Tool Registry, Allowlist, Validation, Dispatch, Results, and Unknown Calls
modules.description: The dispatcher uses globals by name, trusts model arguments, drops call IDs, returns raw exceptions, retries effects, and invokes unknown tools through dynamic import. Learners cumulatively produce allowlisted tool registry and result envelope through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Register immutable tool name, schema version, handler, effect class, policy, timeout, owner, and result contract in one auditable catalog.
Preserve provider call ID, local attempt ID, interaction ID, tool version, arguments digest, result digest, and timestamps.
Validate and normalize model arguments before policy and execution while retaining rejected input evidence safely.

modules.title: User, Agent, and Tool Authority, Least Agency, Dry Runs, and Revocation
modules.description: The model inherits a service account, treats user intent as permission, uses write tools for read tasks, and cannot distinguish allowed, approval-required, or denied actions. Learners cumulatively produce least-agency authorization decision engine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish authenticated user, affected subject, agent, application, tool, service identity, resource owner, and approver.
Issue task-scoped, resource-scoped, time-bounded capabilities and expose only tools permitted for the current state.
Classify pure reads, sensitive reads, reversible writes, irreversible writes, external communication, financial, identity, and security effects.

modules.title: Complete Interactions API Tool Turn and Provider Context Circulation
modules.description: The application reads only output text, misses function-call steps, executes twice, sends a result without call identity, drops provider context, and reports success before final model output. Learners cumulatively produce one complete correlated Gemini tool turn through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify model output, function call, function result, built-in tool, retrieval, safety, error, and terminal interaction steps exhaustively.
Reserve call identity before execution and reconcile retries so one logical function call cannot create duplicate effects.
Return the exact function name, call ID, bounded typed result parts, and required tools and configuration in the follow-up interaction.

modules.title: Explicit Agent State Machine, Progress, Budgets, Termination, and Cancellation
modules.description: A while-true loop stops only when the model chooses text, repeats the same call after unchanged failures, ignores cancellation, and has no terminal reason or partial-result policy. Learners cumulatively produce bounded observable Python agent loop through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent observe, request, classify, authorize, approve, execute, record, continue, complete, refuse, fail, cancel, and escalate transitions explicitly.
Enforce steps, wall time, tokens, model calls, tool calls, retries, bytes, concurrency, and spend before each transition.
Define observable state change and detect repeated calls, unchanged errors, oscillation, cyclic plans, and declining evidence quality.

modules.title: Parallel Tool Calls, TaskGroup Ownership, Ordering, and Shared Effects
modules.description: The agent launches every call concurrently, loses call-result order, mutates shared session state, swallows ExceptionGroup failures, and leaks tasks after cancellation. Learners cumulatively produce structured bounded parallel tool executor through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Prove tool-call independence from inputs, effects, authority, ordering, rate limits, shared resources, and compensating behavior before parallel execution.
Own concurrent calls with asyncio TaskGroup, explicit timeout and semaphore budgets, and structured error and cancellation propagation.
Correlate each result by call ID and return required result parts in provider-valid order independent of completion order.

modules.title: Human Approval, Trustworthy Preview, Pause, Expiry, and Resume
modules.description: The model asks “Are you sure?”, hides exact recipients and amount, edits arguments after approval, treats silence as consent, and cannot safely resume after process restart. Learners cumulatively produce cryptographically bound approval checkpoint through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trigger approval from effect class, value, uncertainty, novelty, policy, affected party, data sensitivity, and user settings before execution.
Render application-owned exact tool, arguments, target, before and after state, consequences, reversibility, cost, and alternatives accessibly.
Bind approval to user, session, policy, tool version, normalized arguments digest, preview digest, expiry, and one execution nonce.

modules.title: Session Events, Atomic Checkpoints, Idempotent Resume, Fork, and Replay
modules.description: Mutable session blobs overwrite history, checkpoints split state from effects, resume repeats a tool call, and a debugging replay contacts production services. Learners cumulatively produce append-only replayable agent session through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define tenant, user, session, invocation, interaction, turn, attempt, call, effect, checkpoint, and parent identities and their lifetimes.
Record typed immutable state transitions with sequence, schema, causation, correlation, redaction, and integrity evidence.
Commit state, pending calls, completed effects, budgets, approvals, and provider references atomically with version checks.

modules.title: Context Assembly, Trust Labels, Token Budgets, Truncation, and Compaction
modules.description: The agent appends everything, places untrusted text beside system instructions, trims oldest messages blindly, trusts summaries, and cannot detect lost constraints after compaction. Learners cumulatively produce budgeted provenance-aware context builder through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Label each context item by source, authority, tenant, purpose, timestamp, sensitivity, trust, instruction status, and expiry.
Reserve output and tool budget, measure or conservatively estimate input tokens, and admit context by task-specific value and risk.
Preserve active policy, user goal, approvals, tool schemas, recent results, unresolved errors, and evidence while removing redundant or expired material.

modules.title: Working Memory, Durable Memory, Write Admission, Provenance, and Poisoning Defense
modules.description: The agent stores every conversation, mixes tenants, remembers injected instructions as facts, never expires data, and treats similarity as truth. Learners cumulatively produce provenance-gated tenant-isolated memory service through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish in-turn state, session memory, user preferences, task facts, organizational knowledge, examples, and audit records by owner and lifetime.
Admit memory only for an allowed purpose with source, claim type, confidence, sensitivity, consent or authority, expiry, and contradiction policy.
Retrieve candidate memories with provenance and policy, then validate relevance, freshness, authority, tenant, and conflicts before context use.

modules.title: Provider and Tool Errors, Retry Eligibility, Idempotency, Compensation, and Stop
modules.description: Every exception receives exponential retry, rate-limit guidance is ignored, write tools repeat after timeouts, compensation is untested, and terminal failures become generic apologies. Learners cumulatively produce typed idempotent agent recovery policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify validation, safety, authentication, authorization, quota, rate, transient provider, terminal provider, tool, policy, timeout, cancellation, and uncertain-effect failures.
Retry only idempotent or protected operations when policy, deadline, budget, status, provider guidance, and attempt evidence permit.
Apply bounded delay, Retry-After, jitter, global and per-resource concurrency, rate, token, and spend budgets without retry storms.

modules.title: Streaming Events, Accessible Status, Cancellation, Reconnect, and Rendering
modules.description: Token deltas overwrite tool status, every chunk is announced, focus jumps, cancel is mouse-only, reconnect duplicates output, and color is the only failure signal. Learners cumulatively produce accessible typed agent event stream through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define typed lifecycle, model delta, tool request, approval, tool progress, result, warning, error, terminal, and usage events with stable identity.
Assemble ordered deltas and state snapshots idempotently while preserving call, interaction, retry, and terminal boundaries.
Announce meaningful milestones through restrained live regions, expose structured text, keep headings and focus stable, and avoid color-only meaning.

modules.title: Prompt Injection, Tool Output Trust, Exfiltration, and Safe Rendering
modules.description: Retrieved text claims to be system instructions, a tool result requests secrets, the agent follows embedded links, approval text is forged, and model Markdown reaches an unsafe renderer. Learners cumulatively produce instruction-data separation and agent security gateway through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent application policy, user instructions, retrieved data, tool output, memory, and provider metadata with explicit authority and non-executable boundaries.
Test direct and indirect goal hijacking, authority spoofing, hidden text, encoded payloads, multimodal instructions, and tool-result injection.
Treat tool names, content, links, files, citations, metadata, error text, and nested model output as untrusted typed data.

modules.title: Secrets, Provider Data Terms, Privacy, Retention, and Sensitive Uses
modules.description: An API key appears in source, prompts contain personal records, free and paid data terms are confused, logs persist indefinitely, and a high-impact use launches without review. Learners cumulatively produce governed secret and agent-data lifecycle through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inject secrets through approved runtime stores, scope and rotate them, prevent prompt or tool exposure, redact derivatives, and destroy test credentials.
Map purpose, legal or organizational authority, fields, sensitivity, users, provider transfer, storage, access, retention, deletion, and aggregation risk.
Record current paid, unpaid, logging, sharing, abuse-monitoring, region, age, and sensitive-use terms and re-review them before release.

modules.title: Representative Eval Sets, Deterministic Oracles, Baselines, and Versions
modules.description: The team tests five hand-picked happy prompts, changes them after failures, has no task distribution or deterministic checks, and compares only final prose. Learners cumulatively produce versioned representative agent evaluation corpus through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Sample normal, boundary, ambiguous, multilingual, accessibility, refusal, tool, failure, adversarial, and recovery cases from the intended task distribution.
Define exact schema, state, tool, argument, authorization, effect, citation, budget, stop, and recovery assertions wherever outcomes are deterministic.
Create changed-input, changed-policy, tool-fault, injection, memory-poisoning, approval, cancellation, and resource-exhaustion cases without leaking answers.

modules.title: Trajectory, Tool Selection, Arguments, Effects, and Stop Evaluation
modules.description: A final answer looks right even though the agent called an unauthorized tool, used wrong arguments, retried a write, skipped an approval, and never reached a valid terminal state. Learners cumulatively produce trajectory-aware agent evaluation harness through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Capture observable state, interaction steps, tool calls and results, approvals, effects, retries, budgets, errors, and terminal reason without hidden-reasoning claims.
Choose exact, in-order, any-order, partial-order, forbidden-step, or state-invariant matching based on task semantics.
Measure required-tool recall, tool precision, unnecessary calls, forbidden calls, schema validity, argument correctness, and result use.

modules.title: Rubrics, Model Judges, Pairwise Comparison, Human Calibration, and Uncertainty
modules.description: A judge model sees model names, produces unexplained one-to-five scores, agrees with itself, replaces human review, and silently overrides deterministic safety failures. Learners cumulatively produce calibrated mixed-method evaluation board through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define observable task-specific criteria, anchors, critical failures, accessibility, safety, evidence, and scoring aggregation before viewing candidate outputs.
Blind model, prompt, provider, and treatment identity; randomize order; use pairwise comparison when it improves judgment reliability.
Measure repeatability, position effects, rubric sensitivity, reference leakage, adversarial manipulation, and judge-version drift.

modules.title: Agent Traces, GenAI Semantics, Token Usage, Cost, Privacy, and Diagnosis
modules.description: Logs contain full prompts and secrets but omit tool arguments, approvals, retries, model version, token usage, cost, and causal links needed to diagnose a failure. Learners cumulatively produce privacy-safe reconstructable agent telemetry through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Correlate service request, session, invocation, interaction, model call, tool call, approval, retry, effect, and recovery spans and events.
Apply versioned OpenTelemetry GenAI operation, provider, model, usage, tool, workflow, error, and output attributes with stability awareness.
Default to metadata and hashes; sample, minimize, redact, encrypt, access-control, retain, and delete prompt, result, and tool content only when approved.

modules.title: Latency, Concurrency, Rate and Spend Limits, Context Cache, and Load
modules.description: The agent parallelizes every request, queues without limit, retries 429s immediately, caches sensitive context indefinitely, and estimates capacity from one local happy path. Learners cumulatively produce budgeted agent capacity and overload controller through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Measure queue, provider time to first event, generation, tool, approval, retry, serialization, and end-to-end latency by outcome and percentile.
Bound sessions, provider calls, tool calls, per-tenant work, queues, task groups, connection pools, and downstream effects with fair admission.
Enforce project and tenant requests, tokens, daily and rolling spend, retry, model, and tool budgets before work starts and while it runs.

modules.title: Google ADK Translation, Runner, Sessions, Tools, Callbacks, and Confirmation
modules.description: The team adopts a framework before understanding its state and effects, duplicates policy in callbacks, trusts defaults, and cannot map framework events back to verified core contracts. Learners cumulatively produce auditable Google ADK adapter and conformance suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Map product policy, provider port, loop, tool registry, session, memory, approval, events, evals, and telemetry to ADK concepts and identify gaps.
Configure Agent, model, instruction, tools, Runner, invocation, events, and async lifecycle with explicit versions and bounded behavior.
Select and test ADK session and memory services for identity, persistence, isolation, migration, retention, and replay boundaries.

modules.title: Multi-Agent Delegation, Context Minimization, Merge, and Failure Containment
modules.description: A coordinator sends full user context and credentials to many agents, gives them shared write tools, accepts unvalidated summaries, and amplifies one poisoned result across the system. Learners cumulatively produce typed contained delegation graph through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use delegation only when specialized context, independent work, isolation, or ownership beats a simpler function, workflow, tool, or single agent.
Define delegated goal, inputs, outputs, non-goals, authority, tools, budget, deadline, evidence, stop, and escalation in a typed contract.
Send only task-required, tenant-authorized, provenance-labeled context and never inherit parent secrets or tools implicitly.

modules.title: Code Execution, Computer Use, Sandboxes, Prompt Injection, and Artifacts
modules.description: The agent executes generated Python on the host, inherits network and credentials, clicks through injected instructions, trusts screenshots, and uploads unreviewed artifacts. Learners cumulatively produce deny-by-default disposable action sandbox through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify generated code as untrusted and define supported language, runtime, packages, inputs, outputs, time, memory, process, file, network, and secret boundaries.
Run code in a disposable isolated environment with deny-by-default capabilities, immutable base, bounded resources, no host mounts, and complete destruction.
Mediate files, environment, network destinations, DNS, uploads, downloads, subprocesses, and credentials through typed policy and explicit approvals.

modules.title: Package, CLI, API, Configuration, Async Lifecycle, and Deployment
modules.description: The agent is one script with import-time clients, embedded keys, no health distinction, inconsistent CLI and API behavior, mutable model defaults, and no graceful shutdown. Learners cumulatively produce installable operable Python agent service through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate domain policy, provider adapter, orchestration, tools, persistence, interfaces, and composition root in an import-safe typed package.
Expose consistent request, event, terminal, error, cancellation, idempotency, and version behavior through accessible CLI and authenticated API surfaces.
Validate environment-specific non-secret configuration, inject secret references separately, reject unsafe defaults, and publish a redacted effective config.

modules.title: Release Gates, Migration, Canary, Kill Switch, Recovery, and Defense
modules.description: A model alias updates globally, SDK behavior changes, offline averages remain green, production incidents lack a kill switch, rollback cannot restore state, and residual risk has no owner. Learners cumulatively produce production agent release and residual-risk defense through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Require schema, unit, trajectory, safety, privacy, accessibility, compatibility, load, cost, deployment, migration, rollback, recovery, and review evidence before release.
Diff model, API, SDK, ADK, schema, safety, tool, token, state, and deprecation behavior against versioned fixtures and representative evals.
Canary immutable candidates against task, safety, tool, refusal, latency, cost, and user signals with predeclared abort and state-aware rollback.
